channel.title: Robin Linacre's homepage: Probabilistic record linkage, Data Science and Data Engineering
channel.description: Probabilistic record linkage, Data Deduplication, Data Science, Engineering and the Environment

item.title: Splink or Swim: The Benefits of Being Small Enough To Fail
item.description: A behind the scenes look at winning the Civil Service innovation award

item.title: Respectful use of AI in software development teams
item.description: How to use LLMs in team settings without damaging team health

item.title: Measuring the accuracy of record linkage
item.description: Some thoughts on how to measure the accuracy

item.title: Using a fault tolerant trie for address matching
item.description: An interactive explanation of how a fault tolerant trie can be used for address matching

item.title: Building Accurate Address Matching Systems
item.description: A bag of tricks to improve the accuracy of geocoding

item.title: Putting Scaffolding Around Vibe Coding to Build More Complex Apps
item.description: How to work past the limits of LLMs to build more complex apps

item.title: Why DuckDB is my first choice for data processing
item.description: Why DuckDB has become my go-to tool for data processing, offering simplicity, speed, and powerful features.

item.title: An alternative way to think about predicted probabilities in the Fellegi Sunter model
item.description: A second equivalent mental model to help think about how we arrive at predicted probabilities in the Fellegi Sunter model

item.title: Live DuckDB WASM Splink model
item.description: A demo of a live Splink model in the browser

item.title: Graph editor for illustrating clustering concepts (graph playground)
item.description: Graph editor for illustrating clustering concepts

item.title: AI probably won't replace me in 2025
item.description: My mental model of LLMs, their strengths and shortcomings

item.title: The emerging impact of LLMs on my productivity
item.description: The emerging impact of LLMs on productivity

item.title: Splink: Transforming data linking through open source collaboration
item.description: How a small team of MoJ analysts built data linking software that’s used by governments across the world

item.title: Connected components visualisation
item.description: A visualisation of how the connected components algorithm works

item.title: Match weight calculator
item.description: A calculator for converting between match weights, probabilities, and Bayes factors

item.title: Super-fast deduplication of large datasets using Splink and DuckDB
item.description: Evaluating 1 billion record comparisons to deduplicate 7 million records in two minutes

item.title: Why Probabilistic Linkage is More Accurate than Fuzzy Matching For Data Deduplication
item.description: How to ensure that all available information is used to make predictions

item.title: Thoughts and questions about the short term impact of LLMs on knowledge workers
item.description: What will be the impact of LLMs on knowledge workers

item.title: Visualising updating a prior
item.description: Using treemaps to visualise updating the prior with information about a scenario in the Fellegi Sunter model

item.title: Computing the Fellegi Sunter model
item.description: A set of interactive, explorable explanations of the Fellegi Sunter model of probabilistic record linkage. This article shows how to compute the model from an algorithmic perspective

item.title: m and u values in the Fellegi-Sunter model
item.description: Deep dive into the role and interpretation of m and u probabilities in the Fellegi-Sunter model for probabilistic linkage. Learn how these probabilities impact match weights and how to quantify the strength of evidence in favor or against a record match.

item.title: Partial match weights
item.description: Partial match weights in the Fellegi-Sunter model. Part of an explorable, interactive introduction to probabilistic record linkage (data deduplication) theory

item.title: The relationship between probabilities, match weights and Bayes factors
item.description: Visualising the correspondence between match weights, probabilities, Bayes factors and their intuitive explanations

item.title: Splink and the Open Source Dividend
item.description: Splink and the open source dividend

item.title: SQL should be the default choice for data transformation logic
item.description: SQL should be the first option considered for new data engineering work. It’s robust, fast, future-proof and testable. With a bit of care, it’s clear and readable.

item.title: Why parquet files are my preferred API for bulk open data
item.description: Open data should be served as CORS-enabled parquet files rather than using a custom API

item.title: Why don't you just
item.description: The phrase 'why don't you just' is problematic

item.title: The Intuition Behind the Use of Expectation Maximisation to Train Record Linkage Models
item.description: An intuitive explanation for how the Expectation Maximisation algorithm is able to produce unsupervised estimates of Splink model parameters

item.title: Splink 3: Fast, accurate and scalable linkage in Python
item.description: Splink 3 now offers support for Python and AWS Athena backends, in addition to Spark. It's now easier to use, faster and more flexible, and can be used for close to real time linkage.

item.title: m and u probability generator with starting values
item.description: Generate m and u probabilities to input into Splink. Part of the introduction to Fellegi Sunter series.

item.title: Are more complex probabilistic linkage models more accurate? Part 2, unsupervised learning
item.description: How good is Splink: Are more complex probabilistic linkage models more accurate?

item.title: Are more complex probabilistic linkage models more accurate? Part 1, supervised learning
item.description: How good is Splink: Are more complex probabilistic linkage models more accurate?

item.title: The Thorniest Problem of Building an Analytical Platform
item.description: The Thorniest Problem of Building an Analytical Platform: Enabling collaborative development of the platform itself without losing control of complexity.

item.title: The carbon impact of switiching to an electric car
item.description: What is the comparative carbon footprint of electric cars? As an existing petrol ICE car owner, should you switch to an electric car

item.title: m and u probability generator
item.description: Generate m and u probabilities to input into Splink. Part of the introduction to Fellegi Sunter series.

item.title: Dependencies between match weights
item.description: An set of interactive, explorable explanations of the Fellegi Sunter model of probabilistic record linkage. The dependencies between match weights.

item.title: Understanding match weights in the Fellegi Sunter model
item.description: An set of interactive, explorable explanations of the Fellegi Sunter model of probabilistic record linkage. This article discusses match weights.

item.title: Visualising the Fellegi Sunter model
item.description: An set of interactive, explorable explanations of the Fellegi Sunter model of probabilistic record linkage. This article presents a way of visualising how the model works.

item.title: Maths of Fellegi Sunter (old version)
item.description: An set of interactive, explorable explanations of the Fellegi Sunter model of probabilistic record linkage. This article shows how to compute the model

item.title: The mathematics of the Fellegi Sunter model
item.description: A set of interactive, explorable explanations of the Fellegi Sunter model of probabilistic record linkage. This article shows the derivation of the mathematical formulation of the model

item.title: An Interactive Introduction to Record Linkage (Data Deduplication) in the Fellegi-Sunter framework
item.description: The first in a series of interactive, explorable explanations of the Fellegi-Sunter model, providing an introduction to probabilistic record linkage (data deduplication).

item.title: The Downfall of Command and Control Data Leadership
item.description: The Downfall of Command and Control Data Leadership - why new big bang data platforms fail

item.title: Demystifying Apache Arrow
item.description: Demystifying Apache Arrow - some observations from a data scientist. Learning more about a tool that can filter and aggregate two billion rows on a laptop in two seconds

item.description: Test how good you are at identifying UK birdsong recordings

item.description: Listen to UK birdsong using the xeno-canto API

item.description: Introducing Splink, a fast, accurate and scalable fuzzy record matching library that supports multiple SQL backends
